articles.headline: RBI tightens rules for recurring digital payments through consolidated e-mandates
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has overhauled e-mandate rules for automated recurring payments to improve transaction processing and transparency. Effective April 21, the rules require an additional factor of authentication (AFA) for registrations and transactions over ₹15,000. Insurance premiums, mutual funds, and credit card bills up to ₹1 lakh are exempt from certain pre-debit alerts.

articles.headline: US Fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh faces intense Senate confirmation hearing
articles.summary: Kevin Warsh, President Trump’s nominee for Federal Reserve chair, faced critical questioning from the Senate Banking Committee on Tuesday. Discussions focused on his institutional independence from the White House following Trump’s public calls for rate cuts. Warsh denied political allegiance, but his potential policy of quantitative tightening is being watched globally for market impacts.

articles.headline: Private doctors remain preferred healthcare choice for most Indians, survey finds
articles.summary: The government’s latest health spending survey for 2025 shows that private doctors and clinics are the most common source of treatment for both rural (43%) and urban (43.7%) Indians. Government facilities see higher demand in rural areas compared to towns and cities. The data is based on a survey of 139,732 households across India conducted by the statistics ministry.

articles.headline: Oil companies halt new LPG connections nationwide due to supply shortages
articles.summary: State-run oil marketing companies have suspended new cooking gas connections for over a month. The halt is linked to supply disruptions caused by the West Asia conflict and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. India imports nearly 65% of its LPG, with 90% sourced from West Asia. Online application systems are currently not accepting new requests while existing supplies are prioritized.

articles.headline: Centre considers stricter AI regulation moving away from light-touch approach
articles.summary: The Indian government is weighing a more prescriptive regulatory framework for artificial intelligence as technology advances rapidly. A newly notified expert committee is preparing guidelines that may differ from previous 'light-touch' stances. Concerns over critical sectors like financial services and energy, alongside deepfake risks, are driving the shift toward coherent AI governance.

articles.headline: Large PMS schemes see performance decline after crossing five thousand crore mark
articles.summary: An analysis of large portfolio management services (PMS) schemes reveals that asset size is becoming a handicap for returns. Five out of six schemes managing over ₹5,000 crore saw performance drop as their investable universe narrowed to liquid large-cap stocks. Experts suggest that smaller funds have an advantage in 'fishing in smaller ponds' like illiquid small and mid-cap stocks.

articles.headline: Bira founder Ankur Jain asked to step down as CEO by investors
articles.summary: Investors and lenders of B9 Beverages, the maker of Bira beer, have requested founder Ankur Jain to resign as CEO as part of a rescue plan. The company has faced a funding squeeze and production halts since September. Existing investors may infuse ₹400 crore at a lower valuation to settle unpaid salaries and vendor liabilities through an out-of-court restructuring.

articles.headline: PwC India evaluates consulting overhaul to align with global operations
articles.summary: PwC India is considering a restructuring of its consulting business to move away from a fragmented national structure toward a global integrated model. The shift aims to standardize methodologies and technology platforms across borders to better serve multinational clients. The audit practice will remain separate due to local regulations.

articles.headline: Government revamps Sugarcane Control Order with penal interest for payment delays
articles.summary: The ministry of consumer affairs has proposed new rules for the sugar sector, mandating mills to pay farmers within 14 days or face a 15% per annum interest penalty. The draft Sugarcane (Control) Order also brings ethanol production within its regulatory ambit and maintains curbs on setting up new mills within a 25-km radius of existing ones to ensure cane availability.

articles.headline: India pivots to high-blend ethanol fuels amid West Asia energy risks
articles.summary: Government officials are negotiating with automakers to roll out engines capable of running on up to 85% ethanol (E85). The move follows energy security concerns sparked by the West Asia war. Additionally, the government is introducing mandatory sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) blending targets, starting at 1% by 2027 and rising to 5% by 2030.

articles.headline: BMI projects India’s FY27 fiscal deficit will exceed budgeted target
articles.summary: Research firm BMI states that India's fiscal deficit is likely to hit 4.5% of GDP in the current fiscal year, surpassing the government's 4.3% budgeted target. The strain on public finances is attributed to the government's policy response to the ongoing West Asia war. The revised estimate for the previous fiscal year was 4.4%.

articles.headline: Centre plans compensation framework for barrier-free highway tolling systems
articles.summary: The government is developing a policy to compensate highway developers for revenue losses that may occur under the new multi-lane free flow (MLFF) tolling system. Physical toll plazas will be replaced by cameras and RFID readers. The compensation plan aims to maintain investor confidence in public-private partnership projects as the system expands to 1,150 plazas.

articles.headline: Government mulls doubling Atal Pension Yojana assured monthly cap to ten thousand
articles.summary: The finance ministry and PFRDA are discussing an increase in the maximum monthly pension under the Atal Pension Yojana from ₹5,000 to ₹10,000. The proposal aims to boost retention and fresh enrolments in the informal sector scheme, which saw 13.5 million new subscribers in FY26. The increase is intended to align the benefit with rising living costs.

articles.headline: Germany and India to sign eight billion dollar submarine agreement soon
articles.summary: Germany expects to finalize an $8 billion submarine cooperation deal with India within the next three months. German Defence Minister Boris Pistorius confirmed the progress of the agreement involving warship maker TKMS and Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders. The deal centers on the construction and technical collaboration for advanced naval vessels.

articles.headline: FSIB opens executive director applications to private sector for first time
articles.summary: The Financial Services Institutions Bureau has invited applications for the post of executive directors at nationalized banks, including candidates from the private sector. This marks the first time the headhunter for state-owned banks has opened these roles to non-public sector professionals. Six vacancies are expected to be filled during the current financial year.

articles.headline: FIEO advises exporters to seek share of US reciprocal tariff refunds
articles.summary: The Federation of Indian Export Organisations (FIEO) has asked members to engage with US buyers to negotiate a share of the reciprocal tariffs being refunded since April 20. While Indian exporters have no legal right to these refunds, FIEO President S.C. Ralhan suggested that strong business relationships could allow exporters to benefit from the payouts received by US businesses.

articles.headline: EAC-PM Chairman expects seven percent GDP growth despite West Asia war
articles.summary: S. Mahendra Dev, chairman of the Economic Advisory Council to the Prime Minister, stated that India's economy is likely to grow 7% this year. He noted that domestic consumption and investment provide a buffer against global shocks. While oil prices may push inflation to 4.5%, it remains within the RBI's tolerance range. Dev highlighted the need for energy source diversification.

articles.headline: Tencent and Alibaba in talks to invest in AI startup DeepSeek
articles.summary: Chinese tech giants Tencent and Alibaba are discussing an investment in DeepSeek that could value the artificial intelligence startup above $20 billion. This would mark the first capital raise for the company, which was previously seeking $300 million at a $10 billion valuation. The move signals continued aggressive investment in AI capabilities by major Chinese firms.

articles.headline: E-commerce platforms drive faster FMCG volume growth than traditional channels
articles.summary: A report by NIQ reveals that online platforms in India are achieving faster volume growth for FMCG brands compared to traditional offline channels. Only 129 out of 64,000 brands were identified as 'volume winners' with over 10% growth. The report notes a convergence in tactics between legacy incumbents and digital-first startups as they scale.

articles.headline: Microsoft to face UK class action trial over cloud licensing claims
articles.summary: A UK antitrust tribunal ruled that Microsoft must face a class action trial involving 59,000 organizations. The lawsuit alleges that Microsoft abused its dominant position to overcharge for Windows Server and Azure cloud services. If successful, the company could face payouts exceeding £1.7 billion ($2.3 billion) to affected business customers.

articles.headline: Nestlé India reports strong Q4 volume growth amid rural expansion
articles.summary: Nestlé India saw operating revenue grow 22.6% to ₹6,748 crore in Q4FY26, with volume and mix growth reaching 20.5%. The performance was driven by an omni-channel strategy, rural distribution reaching 216,000 villages, and a 50% increase in advertising spend. However, analysts warn of potential cooling in FY27 due to rising commodity costs and unseasonal rain affecting wheat.

articles.headline: Public shareholding in listed Indian firms surpasses fifty percent for first time
articles.summary: Public shareholding in listed Indian companies has overtaken promoter ownership for the first time in over a decade. Data from 3,392 firms shows public stakes reaching 50.01% in Q4FY26, while promoter holdings dipped to 49.92%. The shift is driven by domestic institutional investors and retail growth, while foreign portfolio investment hit its lowest level since 2011.

articles.headline: Tata Motors Iveco acquisition faces delays due to EU regulatory review
articles.summary: Tata Motors’ $4.4 billion buyout of Italian automaker Iveco is running behind schedule due to pending approvals from the European Central Bank and the European Commission. Integration plans may be pushed past the initial April-June target. The deal is the Tata group's second-largest acquisition after Tata Steel's takeover of Corus in 2007.

articles.headline: Akasa Air deploys idle planes for Hajj travel amid war disruptions
articles.summary: Akasa Air is utilizing aircraft capacity left idle by the suspension of West Asia routes for month-long Hajj travel to Mecca. About six flights previously serving Doha, Riyadh, and Kuwait are being diverted to Jeddah to maintain fleet utilization. The airline has suspended several international routes until April 30 due to the US-Iran conflict.

articles.headline: India's video streaming revenues surge fifty-five percent in breakout year
articles.summary: Video streaming platform revenues in India grew 55% in 2025, reaching nearly triple 2023 levels. The growth is attributed to regional language content and hybrid revenue models. Digital advertising rose 26% to ₹94,700 crore, while subscription revenues jumped 61% as 143 million households paid for streaming services.

articles.headline: Luggage makers pivot to small bags as travel demand softens
articles.summary: Luggage manufacturers are shifting focus to backpacks and smaller suitcases as high airfares and geopolitical uncertainty dampen peak summer travel. Large suitcase sales have been hit by a moderation in domestic air passenger growth. Companies like VIP and Safari are expanding into non-suitcase segments to protect margins and increase purchase frequency.

articles.headline: Google releases AI agent tools to compete with OpenAI and Anthropic
articles.summary: Alphabet’s Google Cloud unveiled new tools to build AI agents aimed at automating corporate tasks and tracking virtual bot progress. The updates include a memory bank to help agents remember past interactions and tools to test bots before launch. The move is part of Google's $185 billion capital expenditure to justify its massive investment in AI productivity.

articles.headline: Indian pharma firms eye targeted biotech acquisitions in overseas markets
articles.summary: Indian drugmakers like Sun Pharmaceutical and Zydus Lifesciences are increasingly evaluating smaller biotech assets in the US and Europe. Driven by a desire to move up the value chain away from generics, firms are looking for innovative molecules and specialty technologies. Experts project outbound pharmaceutical M&A could reach $20 billion over the next five years.

articles.headline: College incubators under Atal Innovation Mission struggle to scale startups
articles.summary: A decade after its launch, the Centre's Atal Incubation Centres initiative has produced only 6,567 companies, representing just 2.8% of India's recognized startups. Issues include low conversion rates as students prioritize high-paying jobs over risky ventures, a lack of institutional resources, and misconceptions about the long-term nature of deeptech entrepreneurship.

articles.headline: US and Iran delay peace talks in high stakes game of chicken
articles.summary: Negotiations to end the West Asia conflict hit a roadblock as Tehran held back its negotiators, leading the US to pause Vice President JD Vance's trip to Islamabad. President Trump reportedly weighed resuming attacks on Iran while maintaining a naval blockade of Iranian ports. Mediators suggest the delay is a leverage-seeking exercise by Iran's hardline leadership.

articles.headline: Boeing reports lower cash burn and revenue growth in first quarter
articles.summary: Boeing reported a Q1 cash outflow of $1.45 billion, significantly better than analyst estimates of $2.61 billion. Revenue rose 14% to $22.2 billion as commercial aircraft deliveries increased 10% to 143 planes. The company is ramping up production of the 737 family to restore trust and pay down debt, targeting $1-3 billion in free cash by 2026.

articles.headline: SBI Life profit dips one percent as operating expenses and commissions rise
articles.summary: SBI Life Insurance reported a fourth-quarter profit of ₹805 crore, a 1% decline from the previous year. While net premium income rose 16% to ₹27,684 crore, the gains were offset by a 35% jump in operating expenses and higher commissions. Analysts noted that West Asia volatility has weighed on demand for the company's market-linked insurance products.

articles.headline: RBI proposes specific use prepaid wallets and bans cross border transactions
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India issued a draft paper on prepaid payment instruments (PPIs), proposing dedicated wallets for transit, foreign nationals, and gifting. The draft bars PPIs from being used for cross-border transactions and outlines new capital requirements for issuers. The move aims to make PPIs more interoperable with popular UPI apps.

articles.headline: UN warns US Iran war is disrupting vital food aid deliveries
articles.summary: The United Nations’ World Food Programme reported that the US-Iran war and the effective shutdown of the Strait of Hormuz are blocking thousands of tonnes of food aid to famine-hit regions like Sudan. Emergency supplies are stuck at ports in Oman and Kenya with delays reaching 50 days, exacerbating humanitarian crises as shipping backlogs grow.

articles.headline: India textile and agri exports show steady growth despite global turmoil
articles.summary: India's agriculture exports rose 2.8% to $52.55 billion in FY26, driven by fresh fruits and pulses. Textile exports also edged up 2.1% to ₹3.16 trillion. While traditional cotton segments remained flat, ready-made garments and man-made textiles saw modest growth, reflecting sustained competitiveness in a challenging international trade environment.

articles.headline: Japan ends post war pacifist stance by lifting weapon export curbs
articles.summary: Japan has decided to ease restrictions on weapon exports to 17 countries with which it has defense ties, including India. The move marks a significant shift from the pacifist policy adopted after World War II. The change allows Japan to diversify its defense industry and offers India an alternative source for high-tech weaponry amid rising regional tensions with China.

articles.headline: India's micro vacation shift drives revival of weekend trekking trails
articles.summary: Young Indian professionals are increasingly opting for short, intense weekend 'micro-vacations' and treks. This trend is reviving domestic trails in the Sahyadris and Western Ghats. The government has proposed developing sustainable mountain trails in Himachal, Uttarakhand, and Jammu & Kashmir in the latest budget to capitalize on this tourism shift.

articles.headline: Stock Markets Decline as IT Sell-Off Breaks Winning Streak
articles.summary: The Sensex fell 757 points and Nifty dropped 199 points, ending a three-day rally. The slump was primarily driven by an 11% crash in HCL Tech shares following weak guidance. Geopolitical tensions in West Asia pushed crude oil prices near $100 per barrel, raising inflation concerns. Institutional investors, both foreign and domestic, were net sellers during the session.

articles.headline: RBI MPC Minutes Highlight Inflation Risks From West Asia Conflict
articles.summary: Minutes from the April RBI Monetary Policy Committee meeting reveal concerns that the West Asia crisis could drive up energy costs and widen the current account deficit. Members noted that El Nino conditions also pose a threat to inflation targets. The committee decided to maintain a neutral stance and keep the repo rate unchanged to balance growth and price stability.

articles.headline: Manufacturing Wages Decline Relative to Output Value Since FY19
articles.summary: CMIE data shows that manufacturing companies are paying a smaller share of their production value as wages compared to pre-pandemic levels. The wage-to-output ratio dropped from 5.16% in FY19 to 4.97% in FY25. During the same period, company profits jumped 149% while worker compensation rose only 52%. Contract labor now makes up 42% of the total organized manufacturing workforce.

articles.headline: ACME Solar Begins Operation of Battery Storage in Rajasthan
articles.summary: ACME Solar Holdings has commenced operations of a 2 gigawatt-hour per year Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) in Rajasthan. The project is a significant step in strengthening India's renewable energy infrastructure by providing stable power supply. The company's stock rose 2.4% following the announcement, closing at 303.6 rupees.

articles.headline: Havells India Reports 40 Percent Growth in Q4 Net Profit
articles.summary: Havells India saw its net profit surge by 40% year-on-year for the fourth quarter of FY26. The company’s stock reacted positively to the results, closing at 1,348.7 rupees. The growth was driven by strong demand in the consumer electricals segment during the summer season.

articles.headline: Premier Explosives Secures Major Export Order for Defense Supplies
articles.summary: Premier Explosives has bagged an export order worth 350 crore rupees for the supply of defense-related items. The stock jumped 7.5% to close at 535.7 rupees. The order highlights the growing footprint of Indian private firms in the global defense supply chain.

articles.headline: Godrej Industries to Invest 7,000 Crore in Unlisted Businesses
articles.summary: Godrej Industries Group plans to invest between 5,000 and 7,000 crore rupees in its unlisted businesses over the next five years. The investment will focus on Godrej Capital, Godrej Chemicals, and Godrej Vaniish. The group targets a 15% annual sales growth and a 20% growth in earnings per share as it eyes a 5 lakh crore market cap by 2031.

articles.headline: India Real Estate Sector Attracts 5.1 Billion Dollars in Q1
articles.summary: Investment in India's real estate sector grew 72% year-on-year to 5.1 billion dollars in the first quarter of 2026. According to CBRE, institutional investors are increasingly favoring high-quality office spaces and residential projects. Developers led the investment inflow with a 42% share, followed by REITs at 40%. Foreign capital accounted for 90% of the total institutional investment.

articles.headline: Tesla Focuses on Infrastructure and Service Before India Sales
articles.summary: Tesla is prioritizing the establishment of a comprehensive EV ecosystem in India, including fast-charging infrastructure and home charging solutions, before scaling up sales. The company aims to address 'range anxiety' and service concerns. Tesla's Model Y, priced at 61.99 lakh rupees, is expected to strengthen its position in the growing Indian premium SUV market.

articles.headline: Mutual Fund Flexi-Cap Schemes Increase Exposure to Large-Caps
articles.summary: Out of 39 flexi-cap mutual fund schemes, 22 increased their allocation to large-cap stocks by March 2026 compared to the previous year. Managers are citing momentum and attractive valuations in the large-cap segment as primary reasons. Conversely, 17 schemes increased their mid and small-cap exposure, looking for opportunities in sectors like IT, chemicals, and retail.

articles.headline: Indian Rupee Weakens to 93.80 Against US Dollar
articles.summary: The Indian rupee fell to 93.80 per dollar on Wednesday, its lowest level in months, driven by rising crude oil prices and the West Asia crisis. Traders reported that the RBI likely intervened by selling dollars to curb excessive volatility. Higher demand for dollars from oil marketing companies and a general risk-off sentiment in Asian markets added pressure to the domestic currency.

articles.headline: Bandhan Mutual Fund Adds Gold and Silver ETFs to Portfolios
articles.summary: Bandhan Mutual Fund has become the first to utilize new regulatory provisions to include gold and silver ETFs in its equity and hybrid schemes. The fund house integrated precious metals into its Small-Cap and Flexi-Cap funds to reduce volatility and improve risk-adjusted returns. Most schemes have also added 'covered call' options to enhance income generation.

articles.headline: HCL Tech Q4 Profit Rises 4 Percent But Guidance Disappoints
articles.summary: HCL Technologies reported a 4.2% year-on-year rise in Q4 net profit to 4,488 crore rupees. However, its share price crashed nearly 11% as the company projected a modest 1-4% revenue growth for FY27. Analysts highlighted concerns over discretionary spending cuts and the impact of AI on traditional software services pricing.

articles.headline: India's Fiscal Deficit Could Rise to 4.5 Percent in FY27
articles.summary: According to a report by Fitch Group, India's fiscal deficit may rise to 4.5% of GDP in FY27, exceeding the 4.3% target. The increase is attributed to higher subsidy costs for energy and fertilizers amid the West Asia conflict. Government intervention to stabilize the economy through increased social spending and infrastructure inputs is expected to put pressure on public finances.

articles.headline: India Unemployment Rate Projected to Reach 7 Percent in 2026
articles.summary: Moody's Analytics projects India's unemployment rate will rise from 6.9% in 2025 to 7% in 2026, the highest among its Asia-Pacific peers. Inflation is also expected to increase from 2.2% to 4.5% over the same period. The report suggests that high unemployment will persist through 2028, posing a significant challenge to the country's economic growth trajectory.

articles.headline: India Seafood Exports Reach Record 72,325 Crore Rupees
articles.summary: India's marine product exports hit an all-time high of 72,325.82 crore rupees in FY26 despite geopolitical tensions and tariff pressures in the US. However, the Seafood Exporters Association of India warned that this growth might not reflect broad-based strength, citing pressure on value-added products and fluctuations in major international markets.

articles.headline: Coal Production Declines for First Time Since Covid Pandemic
articles.summary: India's coal production saw a 0.5% annual decline in FY26, the first negative growth since the pandemic. In March alone, production fell by 4% compared to the previous year. Experts suggest the dip is due to production hurdles at major producers like Coal India, signaling potential supply-side pressures and price hikes in the energy sector.

articles.headline: Sugar Exporters to Receive RoDTEP Benefits After Court Order
articles.summary: The Mumbai High Court has ruled that sugar exporters are entitled to benefits under the RoDTEP scheme, even if exports were made under special quotas when sugar was in the 'restricted' category. The court stated that the government's denial of benefits was arbitrary. This decision provides significant relief to major exporters like Balrampur Chini and Renuka Sugars.

articles.headline: Indian Air Force Conducts Drills on Purvanchal Expressway
articles.summary: The Indian Air Force has commenced a two-day emergency landing exercise on the Purvanchal Expressway in Uttar Pradesh. Fighter jets landed on a designated air strip to test operational readiness for emergency scenarios. The drill is part of a broader strategy to utilize national highways as alternative runways during conflict or natural disasters.

articles.headline: Iran Attacks Three Ships in Strait of Hormuz Amid US Blockade
articles.summary: Iran fired on three ships and seized two in the Strait of Hormuz, intensifying maritime conflict a day after US President Donald Trump extended a ceasefire but maintained a blockade. Brent crude oil prices rose above $100 per barrel as the European Union warned of daily costs reaching 500 million euros. Separately, a second French soldier died following an attack on UN peacekeepers in Lebanon.

articles.headline: IIT Graduate Raped and Killed in Delhi’s Kailash Hills; Suspect Arrested
articles.summary: A 22-year-old IIT graduate preparing for civil services was raped and strangled with a mobile charger at her South Delhi home. Police apprehended 23-year-old former household employee Rahul Meena in Dwarka. Meena, who was previously dismissed for online gaming addiction, reportedly gained entry while the parents were at the gym. He is also accused of another sexual assault in Rajasthan.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Criticises Mamata Banerjee Over Obstruction of ED Search Operations
articles.summary: The Supreme Court stated that West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee put "democracy in jeopardy" by allegedly obstructing Enforcement Directorate searches at I-PAC offices. The bench described the litigation as extraordinary, noting it went beyond routine Centre-state disputes. The ED's probe involves financial transactions and a coal smuggling syndicate, with the firm’s director currently in custody.

articles.headline: Nepal Home Minister Sudan Gurung Resigns Amid Financial Controversy
articles.summary: Nepal's Home Minister Sudan Gurung resigned following criticism over his financial transactions and undisclosed investments. Gurung, who rose to prominence during recent anti-corruption protests, stepped down to ensure a "fair probe" into the allegations. He is the second cabinet member to leave the newly formed government within weeks, following the dismissal of the labour minister.

articles.headline: Bihar to Distribute Cooking Coal Through PDS Amid LPG Crisis
articles.summary: The Bihar government will distribute coal for domestic cooking through the public distribution system to help 21 million ration card holders. The first-of-its-kind scheme aims to shield 85.1 million people from rising LPG costs and supply disruptions linked to the West Asia conflict. Households can access up to 100 kg of coal per month through biometric authentication at fair price shops.

articles.headline: Government Plans Transition to E100 and Flex-Fuel Vehicles for Energy Security
articles.summary: The Union government is considering E100 fuel blending and a transition to flex-fuel vehicles to reduce dependence on imported crude oil. Officials also announced that the Centre now allows blending in aviation fuel. These initiatives follow the petroleum ministry's gazette notification legally defining aviation turbine fuel as a mixture that can include synthesised hydrocarbons and ethanol.

articles.headline: Delhi Reinstates Rule Requiring Valid PUC Certificates for Fuel Supply
articles.summary: The Delhi government announced that vehicles without a valid Pollution Under Control (PUC) certificate will no longer be supplied with fuel at petrol pumps. The move restarts a policy initially floated last year to combat rising pollution levels in the capital. The rule applies to all fuel stations across the city starting today.

articles.headline: Bombay High Court Discharges Four Accused in 2006 Malegaon Bomb Blasts
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court discharged Rajendra Chaudhary, Dhan Singh, Manohar Ram Singh Narwaria, and Lokesh Sharma in the 2006 Malegaon serial blasts case. The court quashed all charges, including terror, after the accused challenged a special NIA court order. The 2006 blasts killed 31 people. The NIA had previously claimed the blasts were the work of right-wing extremists.

articles.headline: Union Government Overhauling Sugar Sector Regulations to Include Ethanol Production
articles.summary: The government is finalising a sweeping overhaul of sugar sector regulations to include ethanol production and devolve more powers to states. Under the draft Sugarcane (Control) Order 2026, the mandatory distance between mills will increase from 15km to 25km. Ethanol from molasses will be treated as part of the sugar industry, and states will gain authority to approve mill capacity additions.

articles.headline: Online Social Games from Pakistan, China, and Turkey Must Register
articles.summary: The IT ministry notified the Promotion and Regulation of Online Gaming (PROG) Act 2025, requiring certain social games to register based on their country of origin. Registration is mandatory for e-sports, while online money games are banned from May 1. The government may act against games from jurisdictions not aligned with India's foreign policy interests, such as Pakistan and China.

articles.headline: Unregulated Construction Boom Pushes Mussoorie Fragile Ecosystem to the Brink
articles.summary: Unauthorised tree felling and a surge in real estate construction are endangering Mussoorie’s fragile forest ecosystem. Despite court orders and NGT warnings, roads and hotels are being carved out of steep hills where 79% of the area is unsuitable for heavy construction. The town's population density has surged to over 1,600 people per sq km, straining water and waste management systems.

articles.headline: Delhi HC Judge Recuses from Hearing Contempt Plea Against Arvind Kejriwal
articles.summary: Delhi High Court judge Justice Tejas Karia recused himself from hearing a contempt plea against AAP chief Arvind Kejriwal. The petition alleges that Kejriwal and others published videos of court proceedings in the excise policy case that were presided over by Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma. The matter will now be heard by another bench.

articles.headline: Tribunals Clear 139 Names for West Bengal First Phase Voter Rolls
articles.summary: Appellate tribunals cleared 139 names for inclusion in West Bengal's final voter rolls for the first phase of assembly elections. The names were previously flagged under "logical discrepancy" categories. This follows a Supreme Court order allowing the inclusion of cleared voters up to two days before polling. In total, 2.71 million names were deleted during the special intensive revision.

articles.headline: Calcutta High Court Stays EC Directive on Preventive Action Against Citizens
articles.summary: The Calcutta High Court stayed an Election Commission police observer’s order to take preventive action against citizens labeled as "troublemakers." The court held that issuing a blanket direction against approximately 800 individuals, including elected representatives, erred in treating them as offenders without due recourse of law. The stay is effective until June 30.

articles.headline: New NCERT Mathematics Textbook Highlights Ancient Indian Systems and Scholars
articles.summary: A new Class 9 NCERT mathematics book credits the Sindhu-Sarasvati civilisation with the first use of grid-based thinking and Ujjain as the ancient world's central meridian. The text, Ganita Manjari Part 1, links the concept of zero to Indian philosophical traditions like shunya and highlights the works of scholars like Aryabhata, Brahmagupta, and Baudhayana for their roles in modern geometry.

articles.headline: UK Lawmakers Approve Stricter Smoking Ban for Those Born After 2008
articles.summary: British lawmakers approved the Tobacco and Vapes Bill, which effectively bans the legal sale of cigarettes to anyone born on or after January 1, 2009. The legal age for buying tobacco will increase by one year every year to create the nation's first smoke-free generation. The law also tightens regulations on vaping, including advertising and sales to minors, to ease pressure on the NHS.

articles.headline: Eleven Killed in Multiple-Vehicle Collision in Uttar Pradesh’s Mirzapur
articles.summary: Eleven people died in a multi-vehicle accident in the Dramandganj area of Mirzapur on Wednesday night. Police report that a truck descending from a valley likely suffered brake failure and rammed into another vehicle, causing a chain reaction collision. The accident occurred around 8:30 pm, and an investigation into the cause is ongoing.

articles.headline: Indian Student Uses AI to Create MAGA Influencer Persona Emily Hart
articles.summary: A 22-year-old medical student from India earned thousands of dollars monthly by creating an AI-generated MAGA influencer named "Emily Hart." The persona, projected as a US-based nurse, gained millions of views on Instagram by posting pro-Trump and conservative content. The student monetised the account through themed merchandise and subscriptions before it was banned for fraudulent activity.

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu’s Economic Growth Accompanied by Rising Demands for Subsidies
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu has become India’s second-largest state economy, contributing 9.4% to national GDP with per capita income levels 1.7 times the national average. Despite this rise in living standards, political demand for economic populism remains high. The state’s spending on subsidies has consistently been the highest in India, growing faster than in most other major states.

articles.headline: Trinamool’s Ground Visibility Key in Bengal Hinterland as Phase One Begins
articles.summary: Ahead of the first phase of West Bengal elections, analysis suggests the Trinamool Congress (TMC) maintains a major advantage through its deep ground-level presence and problem-solving networks. While the BJP focuses on national narratives, the TMC relies on local panchayat visibility and schemes like "duare sarkar." Rural voters continue to prioritise local political economy over wider issues.

articles.headline: Karnataka Government to Award Marks for SSLC Third Language Papers
articles.summary: The Karnataka government will award marks instead of grades for third language subjects in this year’s SSLC exams, complying with a High Court directive. The court blocked a last-minute shift to a grading system, ruling that evaluation norms cannot change mid-year. Minister Madhu Bangarappa stated the government may seek legal approval to introduce grading for future academic sessions.

articles.headline: Home Minister G Parameshwara Vows Strict Action in Kodagu Rape Case
articles.summary: Karnataka Home Minister G Parameshwara committed to strict legal action regarding the alleged rape of an American tourist at a Kodagu homestay. The primary accused, a cook from Jharkhand, allegedly drugged and assaulted the woman. The homestay owner was also arrested for suppressing the incident and disabling Wi-Fi. The victim eventually contacted the US Embassy from Mysuru.

articles.headline: Two Dead in Head-on Collision Between Car and Pickup in Belagavi
articles.summary: Two people were killed and three others injured in a head-on collision on the Jath-Jamboti State Highway in Belagavi district. The car driver and a passenger died on the spot after the vehicle hit a pickup truck and subsequently crashed into a parked motorcycle. The injured were shifted to a hospital in Gokak, and a case has been registered at the Mudalagi police station.

articles.headline: Bike Taxi Association Seeks Policy Clarity from Karnataka Chief Minister
articles.summary: The Bike Taxi Association submitted a representation to CM Siddaramaiah seeking clarity on the future of operations following reports that the state might challenge a High Court order permitting services. The association noted that thousands of local youth rely on bike taxis for income and urged the government to implement a balanced, legally sound policy framework.

articles.headline: Kannada Actor Ranya Rao to be Released After Completing Preventive Detention
articles.summary: Actor Ranya Rao is expected to be released this week after completing a one-year preventive detention under the Cofeposa Act for her alleged role in a ₹102 crore gold smuggling network. Investigators claim over 100 kg of gold was moved from Dubai to Bengaluru between 2024 and 2025. She remained in custody despite being granted bail earlier due to the nature of the detention order.

articles.headline: Man Arrested in Belagavi for Setting Wife On Fire Over Clothing
articles.summary: A 34-year-old man was arrested in Belagavi for allegedly setting his wife ablaze after an argument over her choice to wear a nightdress at home. The victim sustained 80% burn injuries and was moved to a hospital in Goa for advanced treatment. The incident occurred in Nandgad village, and the accused reportedly doused her in kerosene during the altercation.

articles.headline: Vice President CP Radhakrishnan Criticises Kharge Over Remarks Against PM Modi
articles.summary: Vice President CP Radhakrishnan took a veiled dig at Mallikarjun Kharge, stating the Congress chief fails to distinguish between terror and those who fight it. Speaking in Bidar, Radhakrishnan criticised Kharge's "terrorist" remark against the Prime Minister. The event celebrated the 75th birth anniversary of Dr Basavalinga Pattadevaru Mahaswamiji at Hiremath Samsthan.

articles.headline: Stage Set for Tamil Nadu Assembly Elections Across 234 Constituencies
articles.summary: Over 57 million voters in Tamil Nadu will decide the fate of 4,023 candidates across 234 assembly seats today. Polling will take place at 75,064 booths with 147,000 police personnel deployed. Key candidates include CM MK Stalin, Udhayanidhi Stalin, EPS, OPS, and actor-politician Vijay. The contest is primarily between the DMK-led alliance and the AIADMK-led coalition.

articles.headline: Veteran Politician and TDP Co-Founder Nadendla Bhaskar Rao Dies at 90
articles.summary: Nadendla Bhaskar Rao, who served as the Chief Minister of erstwhile combined Andhra Pradesh for one month in 1984, died in Hyderabad at age 90. Rao co-founded the Telugu Desam Party with NT Rama Rao in 1982 but later led a brief coup against him. He is survived by his son, Nadendla Manohar, an Andhra Pradesh minister and Jana Sena Party leader.

articles.headline: Telangana High Court Stays Action Against KCR in Kaleshwaram Project Case
articles.summary: The Telangana High Court ordered the state government to refrain from coercive action against former CM K Chandrasekhar Rao regarding alleged irregularities in the Kaleshwaram lift irrigation project. The court stayed the operational use of the Justice Pinaki Chandra Ghose Commission report, noting the findings were prejudicial and violated principles of natural justice.

articles.headline: CM Stalin Urges DMK Cadre to Remain Vigilant During Assembly Elections
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu CM MK Stalin appealed to DMK workers to ensure not a single vote is missed as the state prepares for assembly elections. Stalin highlighted the "Dravidian Model" achievements and promised further welfare measures like a ₹2,000 monthly entitlement for women. He also attacked the AIADMK, claiming a vote for them is equivalent to a vote for the BJP.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Vacates Status Quo in Decades-Old Tamil Nadu Land Dispute
articles.summary: The Supreme Court vacated a long-standing status-quo order regarding disputed land in Thazhambur village, providing relief to thousands of homebuyers. The court ruled that a welfare state cannot undo transactions from decades ago at the expense of innocent citizens who invested their savings. The dispute involved land allegedly allotted to freedom fighters and later sold to private developers.

articles.headline: Police Launch Manhunt for YSRCP MLC Ananta Babu Over Witness Bribery
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh police are searching for YSR Congress MLC Ananta Babu for allegedly attempting to bribe witnesses in the Veedhi Subrahmanyam murder case. Police claim Babu offered ₹3 lakh each to four witnesses to change their testimony. A petition has been filed in a special court in Rajahmundry seeking to cancel his bail for violating conditions.

articles.headline: TGSRTC Employees Strike Over Demands for Pay Revision and Merger
articles.summary: Public transport across Telangana was severely disrupted as TGSRTC employees launched an indefinite strike. Demands include pay revision and absorption of staff into the state government. While the government claimed to have accepted 29 of 32 demands, workers continued their agitation. Metro rail services in Hyderabad saw massive crowds as commuters sought alternative transport.

articles.headline: Kerala Government Orders Judicial Probe into Thrissur Fireworks Manufacturing Blast
articles.summary: The Kerala government ordered a judicial probe under retired judge CN Ramachandran Nair into a fireworks unit explosion in Mundathikode that killed at least nine people. The incident was declared a state-specific disaster, with families of the deceased receiving ₹14 lakh in compensation. Initial findings suggest safety rules were violated at the paddy field site.

articles.headline: Election Commission Modifies Bike Ban to Allow Home Delivery Services
articles.summary: The Election Commission modified its restriction on motorcycle movement in West Bengal during the election period. The modified order allows the use of two-wheelers by delivery services such as Zomato, Swiggy, Ola, and Uber, as well as office goers with valid identity cards. Previously, a ban was imposed for 12 hours every night starting two days before each polling phase.

articles.headline: BJP to Intensify Campaigning for Remaining 142 Seats in West Bengal
articles.summary: The BJP will ramp up its campaign for the second phase of West Bengal assembly elections, focusing on state leaders and local language fluency. Prime Minister Narendra Modi is scheduled to hold multiple rallies across the state starting Thursday. The strategy shifts to an intensive ground campaign to sway voters in the 142 seats voting on April 29.

articles.headline: Theni Hill Residents Block EVM Movement Protesting Lack of Infrastructure
articles.summary: Residents of a hill village in Tamil Nadu’s Theni district blocked roads and hindered the movement of EVMs and polling personnel to protest a lack of basic infrastructure. Protesters from Agamalai Panchayat threatened an election boycott, citing long-pending grievances over road connectivity. They alleged that a sanctioned road project has been blocked by forest regulations.

articles.headline: Analysis: Actor Vijay’s Cinematic Journey Shapes His Transition to Politics
articles.summary: As actor Vijay’s TVK party contests its first election, analysis shows his political persona was carefully constructed over decades of cinema. Moving from 1990s romance to socially conscious roles in films like "Mersal" and "Sarkar," Vijay built trust and emotional capital. His direct direct address to fans on systemic change mimics the path of legends like MGR and Jayalalithaa.

articles.headline: Gauhati High Court Delivers Split Verdict on Nagaland Dog Meat Ban
articles.summary: A division bench of the Gauhati High Court delivered a split verdict on petitions seeking a ban on the dog meat trade in Nagaland. One judge dismissed the plea to uphold a 2023 order allowing the trade, while the other judge dissented and sought to quash the 2023 ruling. The case follows a 2020 cabinet decision to ban the commercial import and sale of dog meat in the state.

articles.headline: BJP Delegation Meets ECI Demanding Action Against Kharge for Terrorist Remark
articles.summary: A high-level BJP delegation including Kiren Rijiju, Nirmala Sitharaman, and Arjun Ram Meghwal met the Election Commission to demand strict action against Mallikarjun Kharge. The party described Kharge's "terrorist" remark against the PM as a sign of frustration and hate. Meanwhile, Congress leaders accused the ECI of bias for acting within hours of a BJP complaint while ignoring opposition pleas.

articles.headline: Mumbai Police Book Rally Organisers After Traffic Chaos Video Goes Viral
articles.summary: Mumbai police registered a case against BJP rally organisers for violating rules during a "Jan Akrosh Morcha" that caused major traffic snarls. The action follows a viral video of a woman confronting party leaders after being stuck for over an hour while picking up her child. Former mayor Ritu Tawde expressed regret for the inconvenience but defended the protest for women's rights.

articles.headline: JD(U) Announces National Office-Bearers; Sanjay Kumar Jha Retained as Working President
articles.summary: The Janata Dal (United) announced 24 national office-bearers, retaining Rajya Sabha MP Sanjay Kumar Jha as national working president. Notably, no post was assigned to Nitish Kumar’s son, Nishant Kumar, despite growing speculation about his future leadership role. The list includes 12 general secretaries and eight secretaries, as the party prepares for upcoming political challenges.

articles.headline: Prime Minister Modi Congratulates Indian Cheesemakers on Success in Brazil
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi congratulated Indian artisanal cheesemakers for winning multiple medals at the Mundial do Queijo do Brasil 2026. Mausam Narang’s Eleftheria Gulmarg won Super Gold, while Nordic Farm’s Yak Churpi-Soft from Leh won Gold. Modi stated that these successes strengthen India’s artisanal dairy sector on the international stage.

articles.headline: Supreme Court on Faith and Rights: GUIDELINES Must be Case-Specific
articles.summary: A nine-judge Supreme Court bench indicated it may not lay down uniform prospective guidelines on judicial intervention in religious disputes. CJI Surya Kant cautioned against framing rigid doctrines, stating that questions at the intersection of faith and fundamental rights must be assessed on a case-by-case basis. The bench is examining constitutional questions arising from the Sabarimala case.

articles.headline: Delhi High Court Dismisses Petition Seeking Restrictions on LPG Exports
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court refused to entertain a plea seeking to restrain the export of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) amid an alleged domestic shortage. The court ruled that LPG export is a matter of economic policy falling within the executive's realm and is not subject to judicial intervention. The petitioner had claimed domestic supplies were being compromised by international trade.

articles.headline: Delhi HC Rejects Unnao Rape Survivor Application for Additional Evidence
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court dismissed an application by the Unnao rape survivor to provide additional school records to certify her age. The survivor sought to examine school officials and place a date-of-birth certificate on record. The court rejected the plea for additional evidence at this stage of the proceedings.

articles.headline: NIA Tells Delhi HC Yasin Malik Promoted Kashmir Secession via Pakistan
articles.summary: The National Investigation Agency informed the Delhi High Court that JKLF chief Yasin Malik maintained contact with senior leaders in Pakistan, including its Prime Minister and President. The agency alleged Malik used these high-level links to promote the secession of Kashmir from India and sought to advance a separatist agenda through foreign influence.

articles.headline: Analysis: Information Warfare in West Asia Offers Lessons for India
articles.summary: Analysis of the West Asia conflict highlights the shift of foreign policy crises from diplomatic channels to digital screens. Iran’s use of memes, AI-generated visuals, and social media satire to shape global narratives illustrates how attention is now a primary battleground. India is advised to become a digital first responder to prevent external crises from being defined by others online.

articles.headline: Op-Ed: Assessing Strengths and Weaknesses of Delhi’s Transit-Oriented Development Policy
articles.summary: Urban planning professor Rutul Joshi argues that Delhi’s new Transit-Oriented Development (TOD) policy makes progress on density but fails on diversity and design. By switching to a corridor-based logic, it opens more land for development, but rigid FAR distributions and car-centric parking mandates may hinder walkability and community-specific needs around metro stations.

articles.headline: Op-Ed: Sabarimala Reference Raises Crucial Questions on Constitutional Morality and Religion
articles.summary: A socio-political commentary explores how the Supreme Court's Sabarimala reference could redefine the relationship between faith and law. The authors argue that the "Essential Religious Practices" test has been inconsistent and that the court must balance religious autonomy with constitutional values like equality and dignity without yielding to majoritarian sentiment.

articles.headline: Traders Warn Iranian Strait Tolls Could Set Dangerous Precedent for Trade
articles.summary: Shipping executives at top commodity traders warned that Iran’s attempt to charge tolls for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz undermines the right of "innocent passage." They expressed concerns that this could set a dangerous global precedent, potentially leading to fees at other key maritime chokepoints like Malacca or the Black Sea, disrupting the free flow of global trade.

articles.headline: Iranian Oil Tankers Sail Past US Blockade Using Dark Transponders
articles.summary: At least two Iranian tankers carrying 4 million barrels of oil sailed past a US naval blockade in the Persian Gulf this week. By turning off transponders to avoid detection, a flotilla has shipped roughly 9 million barrels since the blockade began. These movements highlight the challenges in US efforts to completely choke off Tehran’s crude exports to international markets.

articles.headline: US Trade Representative Urges Allies to Pay Premium for Critical Minerals
articles.summary: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer told allies they must be prepared to pay a "national security premium" for critical minerals sourced outside of China. The proposal suggests creating a group of trading partners to secure supply chains for essential minerals, even if it comes at a higher economic cost, to reduce global reliance on Chinese production.

articles.headline: Trump Media Firm Replaces CEO Nunes Following Significant Stock Price Drop
articles.summary: The parent company of Truth Social is replacing CEO Devin Nunes after its stock price plunged 67%, erasing $6 billion in investor wealth. Nunes, a former congressman and close ally of the US President, oversaw the platform during its volatile market performance. The leadership change comes as the company seeks to stabilize its financial position.

articles.headline: Gates Foundation to Cut 20% Staff and Review Jeffrey Epstein Links
articles.summary: The Gates Foundation announced it will cut up to 500 jobs, or 20% of its workforce, over the next few years. In addition to the layoffs, the foundation has initiated an external review of its past engagement with convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. The restructuring marks a significant shift in the operations of the global philanthropic giant.

articles.headline: RBI Consults Global Regulators Over Cyber Risks from AI Model Mythos
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is in talks with global counterparts, including the US Federal Reserve, regarding cybersecurity risks posed by Anthropic’s "Mythos" AI model. Preliminary assessments suggest the model could accelerate software vulnerability exploitation. The RBI is reviewing safeguards and may engage directly with AI developers to protect the financial system.

articles.headline: FAO-WMO Report Warns Heatwaves Threaten Indian Rice Production and Labour
articles.summary: A report by FAO and WMO warns that heatwaves in the Indo-Gangetic plains could reduce agricultural labour capacity below 40% by the end of the century. Rice farming, which is not highly mechanised in India, faces a major threat from compound hot and dry extremes. Historical data shows that major monsoon deficits, such as in 2002, have caused billions in economic damages.

articles.headline: Legacy Coaches Under Scrutiny as T20 Strategies Evolve in IPL
articles.summary: Long-serving IPL coaches Stephen Fleming and Mahela Jayawardene are facing pressure as new playing philosophies and the Impact Player rule transform the league. Teams like Sunrisers Hyderabad and RCB have adopted data-driven scouting and aggressive batting strategies, leading to four different winners in four years. The traditional dominance of MI and CSK is being tested by faster-evolving franchises.

articles.headline: Rajasthan Royals Consign Lucknow Super Giants to Third Consecutive Home Defeat
articles.summary: Rajasthan Royals defeated Lucknow Super Giants by 40 runs at the Ekana Stadium, marking LSG’s third straight home loss. Chasing 160, LSG collapsed to 31/3 in the powerplay and were eventually bundled out for 119. Earlier, Ravindra Jadeja’s unbeaten 43 and Jofra Archer’s 3/20 proved decisive. Mohammed Shami also took two early wickets to dent LSG’s top order.

articles.headline: Golfer Karandeep Kochhar Eyes Success at $2 Million Singapore Open
articles.summary: Indian golfer Karandeep Kochhar is competing in the $2 million Singapore Open at the sapping Serapong Course. Kochhar, who recently regained his Asian Tour card, carries confidence from strong finishes in the Philippines and Japan. The event serves as part of the International Series, offering a pathway to the LIV league and qualification spots for the 154th Open.

articles.headline: Wrestlers Need Tactical and Technical Improvements Says Coach Emzarios Bentinidis
articles.summary: Emzarios "Shako" Bentinidis, former coach of Bajrang Punia, returns as India’s men’s freestyle team coach. Bentinidis emphasised that Indian wrestlers are physically strong but need better tactical focus and mental discipline. He highlighted young talents like Sujeet Kalkal and Aman Sehrawat as future Olympic medal prospects if they refine their technical game on the mat.

articles.headline: Four Boxers Promoted to TOPS Core Group Following Asian Championships Success
articles.summary: Preeti Pawar, Priya, Arundhati Chaudhary, and Sachin have been promoted to the Target Olympic Podium Scheme (TOPS) Core group. The promotion follows strong performances at the Asian Championships, where Preeti, Priya, and Arundhati won gold and Sachin secured silver. The move aims to provide elite support for their training ahead of the upcoming Olympic cycle.

articles.headline: India Women’s Football Team Drawn with Bangladesh for SAFF Championship
articles.summary: The Indian women’s football team has been drawn in Group B alongside Bangladesh and the Maldives for the SAFF Women’s Championship. The tournament will be held in Margao starting May 25. Group A includes Nepal, Sri Lanka, and Bhutan. The top two teams from each group will advance to the semi-finals in the regional competition.

articles.headline: Kylian Mbappe Scores as Real Madrid Defeat Alaves in La Liga
articles.summary: Kylian Mbappe scored his league-leading 24th goal as Real Madrid beat Alaves 2-1 to maintain their slim La Liga title hopes. Vinicius Junior added a second goal from distance early in the second half. Alaves managed a stoppage-time goal through Toni Martinez, but Madrid held on to keep pressure on league leaders Barcelona.

articles.headline: Leicester City Relegated to League One After 2-2 Draw with Hull
articles.summary: Former Premier League champions Leicester City were relegated to the English third tier (League One) following a 2-2 draw with Hull. A late equaliser from Oli McBurnie condemned the Foxes to their second-ever relegation to this level, just ten years after their historic top-flight title win. Leicester remains seven points from safety with only two games left in the season.

articles.headline: Table Tennis Pair Manush and Diya Focus on Asian Games Glory
articles.summary: The mixed doubles table tennis pair of Manush Shah and Diya Chitale has risen to world No. 6 following consistent international success. The duo, who won the WTT Contender titles in Tunis and Muscat, are the first Indian pair to qualify for the WTT Finals. They are now refining their defensive skills and consistency as they target a medal at the upcoming Asian Games.

articles.headline: Godrej Industries targets ₹5 lakh crore market cap by FY31
articles.summary: Godrej Industries Chairman-designate Pirojsha Godrej has set an aggressive goal to triple the group's market capitalization to ₹5 lakh crore by FY31. The 129-year-old group is pivoting toward a performance-led culture with plans to list its chemicals and NBFC units. The strategy targets a compound sales growth of 15% and EPS growth of 20% while building dominant positions in core sectors.

articles.headline: Air India mulls 15-20% flight cuts amid rising jet fuel costs
articles.summary: Following a reported loss of ₹20,000 crore in FY26, Air India is evaluating a 15-20% reduction in flight operations. The cuts are expected to focus on long-haul routes to Europe and North America where operational costs and airspace diversions have impacted aircraft utilization. The move aims to improve network efficiency as aviation turbine fuel prices remain elevated.

articles.headline: Election Commission scrutiny curbs poll season earnings for TN taxis
articles.summary: Taxis and autorickshaws in Tamil Nadu report a significant drop in earnings during the current Assembly election cycle. Strict Election Commission monitoring of campaign spending and a shift toward digital canvassing have reduced bulk orders for transport and traditional garlands. Drivers noted that increased private vehicle ownership among households has also dampened demand for rally transport.

articles.headline: VA Tech Wabag establishes new water treatment subsidiary in Chennai
articles.summary: VA Tech Wabag has incorporated a wholly owned subsidiary, North Chennai Tru Water Private Ltd. The new unit will handle the refurbishment, operation, and maintenance of a 45 million litres per day (MLD) tertiary treatment reverse osmosis plant at Kodungaiyur, Chennai. The subsidiary was formed with an initial paid-up capital of ₹1,00,000.

articles.headline: Energy and intelligence to drive CV industry transformation
articles.summary: Ashok Leyland MD Shenu Agarwal outlined energy transition, intelligence, and social impact as key drivers for the commercial vehicle sector by 2047. Speaking at the CII CV Summit, he advocated for a multi-fuel strategy including green hydrogen, electric, and LNG to reduce dependence on imported crude. He also highlighted AI-driven predictive maintenance as the future 'nervous system' of trucks.

articles.headline: Reckitt India business reports double-digit revenue growth in Q1
articles.summary: Consumer goods major Reckitt announced double-digit revenue growth in its India business for the first quarter of 2026. Performance was led by core brands Dettol and Finish. Despite an uncertain global economic backdrop, the company cited innovation and expanded distribution as primary growth drivers in emerging markets like India and China.

articles.headline: Persistent Systems eyes $2 billion revenue target by FY27
articles.summary: Persistent Systems CFO Vinit Teredesai expressed confidence in reaching a $2 billion revenue goal by FY27 following 16.2% annual growth. The company reported Q4 revenue of $436 million, supported by strong order wins in BFSI and healthcare. While focus remains on organic growth, the firm is evaluating acquisitions to expand its 8-9% revenue footprint in the European market.

articles.headline: Govt allows blending of sustainable aviation fuel in jet fuel
articles.summary: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas has amended Aviation Turbine Fuel regulations to allow blending synthesised hydrocarbons, known as sustainable aviation fuel (SAF). The move aims to decarbonize the aviation sector using feedstock like ethanol and agricultural residue. India targets 1% SAF blending by 2027, increasing to 5% by 2030 to reduce crude oil imports.

articles.headline: Oil Ministry pushes for higher ethanol blending in petrol
articles.summary: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas emphasized that higher ethanol blending is essential to reduce crude oil import dependence, particularly as West Asia conflicts threaten supply routes. Currently, 20% ethanol is blended with petrol. The government is coordinating with automakers to ensure vehicle readiness for higher flex-fuel concentrations.

articles.headline: GAIL to establish robust LNG supply chain with US
articles.summary: State-run GAIL (India) is expanding its liquefied natural gas supply chain from the US to meet rising domestic demand. The company recently flagged off the LNG vessel Energy Fidelity from the Sabine Pass Terminal. This initiative is part of a strategy to diversify procurement sources beyond West Asia and secure sustainable fuel for India's energy transition.

articles.headline: Economic Affairs Secretary urges disaster resilience in infrastructure design
articles.summary: Economic Affairs Secretary Anuradha Thakur emphasized the need to embed disaster resilience into infrastructure projects at the design stage. Speaking at a CDRI event, she noted that global infrastructure losses reach $845 billion annually. Thakur argued that upfront investment in resilience protects public finances and ensures growth continues despite climate risks.

articles.headline: MPC flags supply chain shocks for continuing repo rate pause
articles.summary: All six members of the RBI's Monetary Policy Committee highlighted supply-driven external shocks from the West Asia conflict as the primary reason for keeping the repo rate at 5.25%. Headline inflation is projected to rise to 4.6% in FY27. Governor Sanjay Malhotra noted that while the economy remains strong, constant vigil is required against persistent supply shocks.

articles.headline: OpenAI launches ChatGPT Images 2.0 for advanced image generation
articles.summary: OpenAI has released ChatGPT Images 2.0, an updated model designed for complex creative tasks and professional workflows. The new version features improved instruction following, better text rendering, and support for wide aspect ratios from 3:1 to 1:3. It also includes an API for developers to build image editing directly into their own products.

articles.headline: Dell Technologies launches Intel Core Ultra powered Dell 15
articles.summary: Dell has launched the Dell 15 laptop in India, featuring Intel Core Ultra Series 2 H processors with built-in NPU for on-device AI performance. The device includes a 15-inch anti-glare display and ExpressCharge technology reaching 80% battery in one hour. Prices for the new model start at ₹69,699, targeting users looking for smooth multitasking and efficient AI capabilities.

articles.headline: Sennheiser re-launches CX and HD wired headphones with USB-C
articles.summary: Sennheiser has released the CX 80U in-ear and HD 400U over-ear wired headphones in India, catering to the resurgence of wired audio. The devices support high-resolution 24-bit digital audio with minimal latency, making them ideal for gaming and professional use. The CX 80U is priced at ₹1,990, while the HD 400U retails for ₹5,990.

articles.headline: ASUS makes new Zenbook and Vivobook series available in India
articles.summary: ASUS India announced the availability of its latest laptop lineup powered by Intel Core Ultra Series 3 and Qualcomm Snapdragon X2 platforms. The range includes the Zenbook S14 and several Vivobook models. The Zenbook series starts at ₹1,79,990, while the Vivobook line is available from ₹98,990, targeting both premium and mid-range consumer segments.

articles.headline: PNC Infratech emerges as lowest bidder for ₹3,483 crore NHAI projects
articles.summary: Shares of PNC Infratech surged 6.5% after the company was named the L1 bidder for two National Highways Authority of India projects in Uttar Pradesh. The projects involve constructing a four-lane highway on NH-927 and a stretch from Mustafabad to Biswariya. Total project costs are estimated at ₹3,483 crore with a 24-month execution timeline.

articles.headline: Equitree Capital launches $100 million offshore fund in GIFT City
articles.summary: Mumbai-based Equitree Capital Advisors has launched an offshore fund under the GIFT City framework to offer global investors exposure to Indian small-cap equities. The Category III AIF aims to raise $100 million over the next 12-18 months. The fund targets NRIs, foreign family offices, and institutions seeking listed small-cap opportunities in India.

articles.headline: SEBI panel recommends approval for NSE co-location case settlement
articles.summary: SEBI's advisory committee has recommended approving the National Stock Exchange's settlement application for the long-running co-location and dark fibre cases. The settlement payout is expected to be approximately ₹1,880 crore. Approval would remove a major regulatory hurdle, potentially allowing NSE to file its draft red herring prospectus for a public listing in May.

articles.headline: IT stock sell-off and global tensions drag benchmark indices lower
articles.summary: The Nifty 50 and Sensex snapped a winning streak on Wednesday, dragged down by a sharp decline in IT stocks and geopolitical uncertainty. HCL Technologies led the losers with an 11% drop following weak FY27 guidance. Renewed concerns over stalled US-Iran peace talks also weighed on investor sentiment, although energy and defense stocks managed to close with gains.

articles.headline: OmniScience Capital projects Nifty reaching 31,000 by March 2027
articles.summary: Investment firm OmniScience Capital has projected the Nifty 50 index to hit between 28,000 and 31,000 by the end of FY27. The forecast is driven by expected earnings growth of 10-13% and a structural growth phase in the power sector. The firm remains cautious on IT but sees significant upside in financial services and renewables over the next two years.

articles.headline: Zerodha shuts down Zero1 creator network citing regulatory ambiguity
articles.summary: Zerodha has wound down its creator-led media initiative, Zero1, in response to increasing regulatory scrutiny over financial influencers. The company is shifting to a fully in-house content model for financial education to ensure better investor protection and control. Zero1 had partnered with various influencers to simplify finance and climate subjects through storytelling.

articles.headline: Women's reservation can be implemented after 2027 Census delimitation
articles.summary: Despite the recent defeat of new amendments in the Lok Sabha, the 2023 Women's Reservation Act remains law. The quota can theoretically be implemented following the first census post-2026 and the subsequent delimitation process, likely in time for the 2034 general elections. Implementation for the 2029 polls would require new legislation delinking the process from delimitation.

articles.headline: Germany plans to build new gas-fired power plants from September
articles.summary: Germany will hold tenders starting in September to build 9 gigawatts of new gas-fired power plants to ensure energy flexibility as coal is phased out. The draft law requires these plants to be operational by 2031 and capable of switching to hydrogen. The project faces political pressure regarding financing and potential setbacks for renewable energy targets.

articles.headline: West Bengal Phase 1 polls to be held under tight vigil
articles.summary: Over 3.6 crore voters in 152 constituencies across 16 districts will participate in the first phase of West Bengal's Assembly elections. The Election Commission has banned motorcycle use and increased security in areas with high voter list deletions. Key issues include jobs, women's safety, and alleged corruption, with the BJP and Trinamool Congress in a high-voltage contest.

articles.headline: TMC alleges poll code violation by Suvendu Adhikari in Bengal
articles.summary: The Trinamool Congress has filed a complaint with the West Bengal Chief Electoral Officer against BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari for allegedly violating time regulations during a rally. The TMC claims Adhikari's meeting in South 24 Parganas exceeded its allotted time by several hours and disrupted pre-approved party events. The party also accused police conduct of being partisan.

articles.headline: Udhayanidhi Stalin faces seasoned challenger in DMK stronghold Chepauk
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu Deputy CM Udhayanidhi Stalin aims to increase his victory margin in the Chepauk-Thiruvallikeni constituency. While the seat is a historical DMK fortress, he faces AIADMK's Aadi Rajaram. Residents have highlighted local issues such as stray cattle, traffic congestion, and the need for better waste management near the Tiruvallikeni MRTS station.

articles.headline: Tiruppur textile cluster seeks infrastructure upgrades as poll issue
articles.summary: Textile entrepreneurs in Tiruppur, India's knitwear capital, are demanding better roads and waste management from candidates in the upcoming elections. Despite annual garment manufacturing worth ₹70,000 crore, the town faces severe sanitation challenges and a 20% labour shortage. High electricity charges and property tax hikes remain primary grievances for the industry.

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu set for polls as 5.73 crore voters choose today
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu goes to the polls on April 23 in a three-cornered contest involving the DMK, AIADMK, and the Tamil Vettri Kazhagam. The Election Commission has deployed 3.6 lakh personnel across 75,064 polling stations. Authorities have already seized ₹543 crore in cash ahead of the vote. First-time voters account for 14.59 lakh of the total 5.73 crore electoral strength.

articles.headline: Affidavits reveal stark wealth disparities among Tamil Nadu candidates
articles.summary: Analysis of candidate filings shows AIADMK's Leema Rose Martin as the wealthiest contestant in TN with assets over ₹5,800 crore. Actor-turned-politician C Joseph Vijay follows with ₹426.2 crore in movable and ₹222.6 crore in immovable assets. Conversely, several candidates listed minimal holdings, highlighting the economic diversity within the state's electoral field.

articles.headline: SC: CM walking into raid is not a Centre-State dispute
articles.summary: The Supreme Court clarified that West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee's conduct of walking into an ongoing ED raid in January cannot be treated as a Centre-State dispute under Article 131. Justice Prashant Kumar Mishra observed that such actions by an individual do not constitute a legal dispute between governments. The ED is seeking a CBI probe into the incident.

articles.headline: Govt urea stock drops to four-year low ahead of Kharif season
articles.summary: India's opening stock of urea as of April 1 fell to 54.22 lakh tonnes, the lowest in four years. This inventory dip poses a risk to crop yields if monsoon rains are below normal. While other fertilizer stocks remain comfortable, the government is aggressively importing urea to ensure adequate supplies for the upcoming sowing season which begins with the monsoon.

articles.headline: Extreme heat report warns of rising threats to agri-food systems
articles.summary: A report by the FAO and WMO warns that intensifying heat events are creating systemic risks for global agriculture. The number of days too hot for outdoor work could rise to 250 per year in South Asia. Extreme heat acts as a risk multiplier for wildfires and flash droughts, necessitating innovation in selective breeding and social protection schemes to help farmers adapt.

articles.headline: India to import 2.5 million tonnes of urea to secure supply
articles.summary: Indian Potash (IPL) will import 2.5 million tonnes of urea at $935 per tonne for West Coast and $959 per tonne for East Coast delivery. To avoid the conflict-prone Strait of Hormuz, supplies will be sourced from Russia, Algeria, Nigeria, Egypt, and Indonesia. This tender reflects an 84% price increase compared to mid-February as logistical challenges mount.

articles.headline: Indian diamond industry faces most severe crisis in 60 years
articles.summary: India's diamond industry is experiencing a prolonged contraction, with exports in FY26 dropping to $12.16 billion, nearly half the peak of $24.24 billion in FY22. Rajya Sabha MP Govind Dholakia attributed the downturn to structural confusion caused by lab-grown diamonds and global economic uncertainty. However, green shoots are emerging as prices for larger carats begin to recover.

articles.headline: SBI report flags lower assistance scheme utilization in ageing states
articles.summary: An SBI research report found that 'ageing' states like Kerala and Tamil Nadu use less of the Special Assistance to States for Capital Investment (SASCI) compared to youthful states. The report noted that some states substitute their own capital expenditure with SASCI funds rather than creating new investment. Fiscal deficits for states are expected to reach 3.2% by FY27.

articles.headline: Medical device body AiMed calls for caps on product mark-ups
articles.summary: The Association of Indian Medical Device Industry (AiMed) has urged for a multi-stakeholder dialogue to define ethical markups across the supply chain. The body supports capping hospital billing at 50% over procurement costs to prevent profiteering. AiMed argues that rational price corrections on essential devices will reduce insurance premiums and widen healthcare coverage.

articles.headline: Railways delivers first bulk Amul milk consignment to J&K
articles.summary: Indian Railways has transported its first bulk freight consignment of Amul milk and dairy products to Jammu and Kashmir. The special train, comprising 20 wagons, delivered toned milk and powder to the Bari Brahman and Anantnag stations. The move is intended to ensure a steady supply of essential dairy items while reducing transportation costs and delivery times in the region.

articles.headline: Agri Minister reviews low wheat procurement in Vidisha
articles.summary: Union Agriculture Minister Shivraj Singh Chouhan directed collectors to speed up wheat purchases following farmer dissatisfaction in Vidisha. Discrepancies in satellite-based crop verification and server issues have allegedly prevented some farmers from selling their produce. Madhya Pradesh has reported procurement of only 78 lakh tonnes this season, marginally higher than the previous year.

articles.headline: Cognizant and Infosys partner with OpenAI to scale enterprise AI
articles.summary: IT majors Cognizant and Infosys have separately announced strategic collaborations with OpenAI. Cognizant will embed OpenAI's Codex into its software engineering workflows for clients, while Infosys will help enterprises modernize software development using frontier AI models. Both partnerships aim to accelerate digital transformation and AI-driven efficiency for global businesses.

articles.headline: Amazon India partners with law enforcement to curb counterfeits
articles.summary: Amazon is expanding its Counterfeit Crimes Unit (CCU) in India to combat fake products through AI and litigation. Working with the CBI and the Indian Cybercrime Coordination Centre, the company helped prevent transactions worth ₹17.7 crore last year. Amazon aims to protect both Indian consumers and brands by fabricators and distributors of counterfeit goods.

articles.headline: Mphasis acquires Canadian AI firm Theory and Practice for ₹69 crore
articles.summary: IT firm Mphasis has acquired Canada-based Theory and Practice (TAP) for an upfront consideration of CAD 10 million. TAP specializes in decision intelligence and behavioral economics for financial and retail sectors. Mphasis plans to integrate TAP’s Continuum AI platform to provide enterprises with advanced demand forecasting and revenue optimization capabilities.

articles.headline: Global IT spending projected to grow 13.5% to $6.3 trillion
articles.summary: Gartner data forecasts that worldwide IT spending will reach $6.31 trillion in 2026, a 13.5% increase from the previous year. The growth is primarily driven by investments in data center systems for AI infrastructure and advanced memory. Software spending is expected to rise by 15.1%, reflecting a shift from cautious spending to expansion-driven investment cycles.

articles.headline: Iran Polices Hormuz Strait After Trump Puts Off Attacks
articles.summary: Iran’s Revolutionary Guards have seized two vessels for maritime violations in the Strait of Hormuz after US President Donald Trump called off planned attacks. The move follows a period of heightened tensions. Washington reports the seized ships were likely headed for Mundra port in India. The region remains a strategic choke point for global oil and maritime trade.

articles.headline: Indian AIFs in Gulf Star at Sea of Uncertainty
articles.summary: Indian Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) in the Gulf region are facing uncertainty as high-net-worth individuals and sovereign investors pause capital inflows. Fund managers are being forced to redirect strategies due to regional conflicts. Fresh commitments have slowed significantly as investors adopt a wait-and-watch approach toward India-focused venture capital and private equity.

articles.headline: Inox Clean May Buy Out Boviet at $750m Enterprise Value
articles.summary: Inox Clean Energy is set to acquire Boviet Solar in a deal valued at $750 million. The move aims to establish a presence for the Noida-based renewable energy company in the US market.
